level_signals: 5-6+ years experience in consulting, investment banking/private equity, or a fast-growing startup
Comp range $185K-$215K base consistent with Manager/Sr Manager level
Title range explicitly spans Manager and Sr Manager

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree in Business, Economics, or related field
credentials_named: Bachelor's degree in Business, Economics, or related field

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5-6+ years experience in consulting, investment banking/private equity, or a fast-growing startup
Able to move remarkably fast with little structure, guidance
Curious to learn about different parts of the business and how to pull certain levers to grow faster
Ability to operate independently and go deep on ambiguous projects

Demonstrated ability to work and influence cross-functionally. You are proactive, great at communicating with different audiences and can bring stakeholders on-board. You seek out and have a track record for driving change and impact in a fluid, unstructured working environment
Fluency in using AI tools to enhance problem-solving, analysis, and execution across a wide range of business challenges

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience working on a marketplace or a two-sided platform
Experience or passion for buying and selling in online marketplace communities

scope.surface: Cross-functional business operations: international expansion, marketing investment rigor, margin/growth analysis, internal tooling, and user-facing operations
core_responsibilities: Lead large, critical cross-functional initiatives and solve unstructured problems
Support international market scaling across UK, France, Germany, Canada
Conduct primary research to identify high-impact opportunities across product, pricing, and incentives
Own key metrics for international markets including buyer/seller funnels, growth, marketing investments, and margin dynamics
Bring business-side rigor to marketing capital deployment, measurement, and prioritization
Partner with StratFin and Data on margin levers, growth investments, and pricing decisions
Build tooling as a single source of truth for tracking progress against goals
Pressure-test new products and capabilities, translate learnings into initiatives improving buyer/seller experience

green_flags: Fully remote role with global hub flexibility
Clear mandate and concrete initiative examples provided
High-visibility role working directly with leadership team
Named #1 Best Startup Employer in America by Forbes — external validation
Strong benefits package including parental leave, 401k match, wellness, childcare allowance
red_flags: Title spans Manager and Sr Manager levels — leveling and final comp will vary significantly depending on experience
Highly ambiguous, unstructured role expectations may be challenging without prior startup or consulting experience
No explicit mention of direct reports — people management expectations are unclear

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and title span)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities, not explicitly listed as KPIs)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from comp stated in USD and 'remote co-located' description)
ambiguities: The JD describes Whatnot as a 'remote co-located team' with hubs across multiple countries, but does not explicitly state whether this specific role is remote-global or US-remote-only. Treated as remote-global given the international hub list and no geographic restriction on the role.
The salary range note states it 'may be inclusive of several levels' — unclear whether $185K maps to Manager and $215K to Sr Manager or if both levels fall within the full range.
